description:
Cross-language consistency fixture for the SelfVerifying loop strategy (issue #61).

Each case scripts a sequence of verifier verdicts (`verdicts`: "pass" means Passed, any other string is a Failed reason) replayed across the build<->evaluate round-trips, the verifier's `max_iterations` cap, and the expected terminal RunResult. The harness build/evaluate agents are scripted to always claim done, so the verdict sequence fully determines the outcome:
  - the first `pass` verdict at iteration i => Success.
  - all verdicts Failed up to `max_iterations` => Failure { SelfVerifyExhausted { iterations: max_iterations } } (the stagnation guard / Default-FAIL contract).
  - `misconfigured` cases run with NO verifier configured => Failure { SelfVerifyMisconfigured }.

Expected.kind is one of: success | exhausted | misconfigured. `iterations` documents the round-trip count.
